value: Ohio: Ohio Department of Natural Resources (ODNR) — Orphan Well Program (BIL/IIJA-funded)
Pennsylvania: Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection (PA DEP) — Orphan and Abandoned Well Plugging Program (BIL/IIJA-funded)
West Virginia: West Virginia Department of Environmental Protection (WVDEP) — Abandoned Well Program (BIL/IIJA-funded)
Kentucky: Kentucky Energy and Environment Cabinet — Kentucky Abandoned/Orphan Well Plugging Program (BIL/IIJA-funded)
Orphan Well Cooperative — nonprofit hub with explicit report-orphan-well field + public prioritization matrix
Well Done Foundation — adopt-a-well + carbon; ~$65k/well; methane-first selection
Rebellion Energy — for-profit carbon, OK-centric, high-emission wells
Zefiro Methane — acquires via state RFP bids only
EDF — not a plugger - bulk data source (120k-well dataset)
ACR Orphaned Well Methodology (2023): plugging a high-emitter can mint carbon credits — Zefiro ACR959 = 92,956 t CO₂e from ONE well (sold to Mercuria, 2025).
WV/OH/KY parcels are free via ArcGIS REST point-in-polygon (no key); WV uses WV_Parcels FullOwnerName. PA needs a county service or national-API trial.
Federal BIL/IIJA orphan-well fund is $4.7B; Justice40 prioritizes disadvantaged (CEJST) communities for set-asides.
